statement: Limestone is a sedimentary rock composed primarily of calcium carbonate.
quizQuestion: What is limestone primarily made of?

variants.question: What chemical compound forms limestone?
Which sedimentary rock contains calcium carbonate?
Which is NOT a sedimentary rock?
Limestone is formed from calcium carbonate in _____ environments.
Limestone can be used as building material.

wowFactor: Limestone shells and skeletons turned to rock!
visualDescription: A cross-section of layered limestone cliff showing sedimentary bands in cream and gray. Fossil shells and coral fragments visible within the rock layers. Sunlight casts shadows on the textured stone surface.
explanation: Limestone shells and skeletons turned to rock!

statement: Bronze is a metal alloy made from copper and tin.
quizQuestion: What two metals make up bronze?

variants.question: Which metals combine to form bronze?
Name the metal alloy of copper and tin.
Which is NOT a component of bronze?
Bronze is an alloy of copper and _____.
Bronze was widely used in ancient weaponry and tools.

wowFactor: Ancient warriors wielded bronze swords stronger than pure copper!
visualDescription: A bronze shield gleaming in sunlight, with swirling metallic patterns. Beside it, raw copper and tin ingots glow warmly. Pixelated hammers and anvils frame the scene.
explanation: Ancient warriors wielded bronze swords stronger than pure copper!

statement: Bambuseae is a tribe of bamboo plants in the Bambusoideae subfamily.
quizQuestion: What taxonomic rank is Bambuseae?

variants.question: Bambuseae belongs to which subfamily?
Which group contains Bambuseae?
Which is NOT a bamboo subfamily?
Bambuseae is a _____ of bamboo plants.
Bambuseae plants are grasses.

wowFactor: Bamboo plants have their own tribal classification in plant taxonomy!
visualDescription: A pixel-art forest scene with tall green bamboo stalks towering over a misty landscape. Golden sunlight filters through the dense canopy, illuminating a botanical classification chart in the corner showing Bambuseae's position.
explanation: Bamboo plants have their own tribal classification in plant taxonomy!

statement: Copper-based alloys contain copper as their principal metal component.
quizQuestion: What metal is principal in copper-based alloys?

variants.question: Copper-based alloys have copper as what?
What is an alloy with copper as principal metal?
Which is NOT a copper-based alloy characteristic?
A _____ has copper as its main metal component.
Copper-based alloys always contain copper.

wowFactor: Copper alloys like bronze and brass shaped ancient civilizations!
visualDescription: A glowing copper coin dissolving into swirling streams of brass and bronze, with flickering firelight reflecting off metallic surfaces in a medieval blacksmith's workshop.
explanation: Copper alloys like bronze and brass shaped ancient civilizations!

statement: Tool steel is carbon and alloy steel designed for manufacturing cutting tools.
quizQuestion: What is tool steel primarily used for?

variants.question: What materials compose tool steel?
Carbon and alloy steel used in tool making.
Which is NOT a tool steel component?
Tool steel hardens well due to high _____ content.
Tool steel is harder than stainless steel.

wowFactor: Tool steel is so hard it can cut through almost anything—that's why it builds the tools that build everything else!
visualDescription: A forge glowing orange-red, with a steel blade being hammered on an anvil. Sparks fly upward. Tools hang on wooden pegs: chisels, wrenches, cutting dies. Crystalline carbon structures shimmer inside the metal.
explanation: Tool steel is so hard it can cut through almost anything—that's why it builds the tools that build everything else!

statement: Phosphogypsum is calcium sulfate hydrate formed during phosphate fertilizer production.
quizQuestion: What is phosphogypsum primarily composed of?

variants.question: Phosphogypsum forms as a by-product of what industrial process?
What material is produced when processing phosphate rock for fertilizer?
Which is NOT a component of phosphogypsum?
Phosphogypsum is a _____ formed during fertilizer production.
Phosphogypsum contains water in its crystalline structure.

wowFactor: A giant white mountain of waste: phosphogypsum stacks billions of tons globally from fertilizer factories!
visualDescription: Industrial phosphate mining facility with towering white phosphogypsum stacks under blue sky. Conveyor belts carry sulfate crystals. Steam rises from processing tanks. Mining equipment scattered across dusty landscape.
explanation: A giant white mountain of waste: phosphogypsum stacks billions of tons globally from fertilizer factories!

statement: Bainite is a microstructure that forms in specially treated iron alloys.
quizQuestion: What is bainite in metallurgy?

variants.question: What microstructure forms in treated iron?
Iron microstructure formed by heat treatment?
Which is NOT an iron microstructure?
_____ forms when iron cools at moderate rates.
Bainite is a phase in steel microstructures.

wowFactor: Bainite is the 'Goldilocks' microstructure—formed at just the right cooling speed!
visualDescription: A microscopic steel cross-section showing feathery bainite crystals in dark gray, interspersed with lighter iron phases. Industrial furnace glowing in background. Crystalline texture under magnification.
explanation: Bainite is the 'Goldilocks' microstructure—formed at just the right cooling speed!

statement: Petroleum seeps are natural locations where hydrocarbons escape from underground.
quizQuestion: What is a petroleum seep?

variants.question: Where do hydrocarbons naturally escape to surface?
Natural location where underground hydrocarbons emerge?
Which is NOT a petroleum seep characteristic?
A petroleum _____ is where oil naturally leaks out.
Petroleum seeps are artificially constructed by humans.

wowFactor: Earth naturally bleeds oil through cracks in the ground!
visualDescription: Pixel-art ocean floor with dark crude oil bubbles rising from rocky seafloor crevices. Swirling brown-black liquid streams upward through blue-green water toward surface light.
explanation: Earth naturally bleeds oil through cracks in the ground!

statement: Architectural glass is a transparent building material used in window glazing.
quizQuestion: What is architectural glass primarily used for?

variants.question: What is the main purpose of architectural glass?
Which material is used as transparent glazing in buildings?
Which is NOT a building material for glazing?

Architectural glass is always opaque and non-transparent.

wowFactor: Glass lets light through buildings while keeping weather out!
visualDescription: A modern skyscraper with shimmering glass windows reflecting clouds. Transparent panes arranged in a grid pattern form the building envelope, allowing sunlight to pour through.
explanation: Glass lets light through buildings while keeping weather out!

statement: Baumberger Kalksandstein is a German limestone-sandstone building material.

Which is NOT a use for Baumberger Kalksandstein?

Baumberger Kalksandstein combines limestone and sandstone properties.

wowFactor: A hybrid stone combining two minerals, Baumberger Kalksandstein built German architectural heritage!
visualDescription: A golden-tan stone block with visible grain texture, stacked with others in a medieval German cathedral wall, sunlight casting shadows on carved architectural details.
explanation: A hybrid stone combining two minerals, Baumberger Kalksandstein built German architectural heritage!

statement: Stoneware is a vitreous ceramic made from stoneware or fire clay.

variants.question: What is stoneware primarily composed of?

Which is NOT a stoneware characteristic?

wowFactor: Ancient stoneware pottery outlasts earthenware by centuries!

explanation: Ancient stoneware pottery outlasts earthenware by centuries!

statement: Reinforcement strengthens concrete by resisting tensile forces and cracks.

variants.question: Why is reinforcement added to concrete?

wowFactor: Hidden steel bars make skyscrapers possible!
visualDescription: Cross-section of concrete block showing steel rebar grid embedded inside gray concrete, with visible cracks stopped by the reinforcing bars.
explanation: Hidden steel bars make skyscrapers possible!

statement: Dross is impurities that form on molten metal surfaces.
quizQuestion: What is dross in metalworking?

variants.question: What is dross in metalworking?

wowFactor: Metalworkers skim crusty impurities off liquid metal like foam off broth!

explanation: Metalworkers skim crusty impurities off liquid metal like foam off broth!

explanation: German engineering gave the world a specialized plastic term!

explanation: Ancient desert sands cemented into solid rock through deep burial!

quizQuestion: What part of a tree does bark protect?

variants.question: What is bark's main function?
Which tissue covers tree trunks?
Which is NOT a bark function?
Bark grows outside the _____ cambium.
Cork comes from the bark of oak trees.

wowFactor: Some redwood bark is over 30 centimeters thick!
visualDescription: A massive old-growth tree trunk with deeply furrowed rough brown bark. A cross-section diagram shows bark layers from outer dead cork to inner living phloem tissue.
explanation: Some redwood bark is over 30 centimeters thick!

statement: Polyester is a category of synthetic polymers widely used in textiles.
quizQuestion: What type of material is polyester?

variants.question: What is polyester commonly used for?
Which synthetic fiber dominates clothing?
Which fabric is NOT synthetic?
Polyester is a _____ polymer fabric.
Polyester is the world's most used textile fiber.

wowFactor: Over 50 percent of all clothing worldwide contains polyester!
visualDescription: Rolls of vibrant polyester fabric in various colors and patterns stacked in a textile factory. Microscopic view inset shows smooth uniform synthetic fibers compared to rough natural cotton.
explanation: Over 50 percent of all clothing worldwide contains polyester!

statement: Wallpaper is a decorative material applied to interior walls.
quizQuestion: Where is wallpaper typically applied?

variants.question: What is wallpaper used for?
Which material decorates interior walls?
Which is NOT a wall decoration?
Wallpaper covers and decorates _____ walls.
Wallpaper originated in China around 200 BC.

wowFactor: Victorian wallpaper sometimes contained deadly arsenic pigments!
visualDescription: A half-decorated room showing ornate floral wallpaper being applied to a white wall. Rolls of patterned wallpaper in various designs lean against the baseboards with paste and brushes.
explanation: Victorian wallpaper sometimes contained deadly arsenic pigments!

statement: Planck time is the smallest meaningful unit of time in physics.
quizQuestion: What is the smallest unit of time called?

variants.question: What does Planck time represent?
Which is physics' smallest time measure?
Which is NOT a Planck unit?
_____ time is the shortest describable duration.
Nothing can happen faster than one Planck time.

wowFactor: A second contains more Planck times than seconds since the Big Bang!
visualDescription: An abstract visualization of time scales from cosmic clock down to subatomic oscillations. A spiral timeline zooms from galaxies through atoms to the quantum foam at Planck scale.
explanation: A second contains more Planck times than seconds since the Big Bang!

statement: Planck length is the smallest meaningful unit of length in physics.
quizQuestion: What is the smallest unit of length called?

variants.question: What does Planck length represent?
Which is physics' smallest distance unit?
Which is larger than Planck length?
_____ length is the tiniest measurable distance.
Planck length is smaller than any atom.

wowFactor: If an atom were universe-sized, Planck length would be tree-sized!
visualDescription: A dramatic scale comparison showing the universe shrinking through galaxies stars atoms and quarks to the tiny Planck length. Nested circles illustrate each magnitude of scale.
explanation: If an atom were universe-sized, Planck length would be tree-sized!

statement: Angelica archangelica is a medicinal herb used in traditional remedies.
quizQuestion: What type of plant is Angelica archangelica?

variants.question: What is Angelica archangelica used for?
Which herb has species name archangelica?
Which is NOT a medicinal herb?
Angelica archangelica is a _____ plant.
Angelica is used to flavor gin and liqueurs.

wowFactor: Legend says an angel revealed angelica's healing powers to a monk!
visualDescription: A tall Angelica archangelica plant with large umbrella-shaped flower clusters in green and white. The robust hollow stems and broad leaves grow beside dried medicinal preparations.
explanation: Legend says an angel revealed angelica's healing powers to a monk!
